title: Pigs in Blankets
subtitle: Chipolata sausages wrapped in streaky bacon, roasted until crisp
excerpt: Pigs in blankets are small pork sausages wrapped in a rasher of streaky bacon and roasted in a hot oven until the bacon is crisp and the sausage is caramelised underneath. They take 25 to 30 minutes in a hot oven. They are the most fought-over thing on the Christmas plate and the first to run out.
sourceNotes: Traditional British Christmas and Sunday roast accompaniment. Pigs in blankets in the British form (sausage wrapped in bacon) are a twentieth-century tradition, now standard on the Christmas table. Distinct from the American version (sausage in pastry).

content.text: There is very little to say about pigs in blankets technically. The key points are that the bacon must be stretched before wrapping to make it pliable enough to wrap tightly without tearing, and that the sausages should be placed join-side down in the pan so the bacon seam is held closed by the weight of the sausage. Chipolatas are the standard sausage; ordinary pork sausages cut in half work equally well.

Heat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan). Stretch each rasher of {{streaky-bacon}} bacon by running the flat of a knife along its length. Wrap one rasher around each {{chipolata-sausages}} chipolata sausage, stretching the bacon as you go so it overlaps slightly at the join. Place join-side down in a single layer in a roasting pan. Roast for 25 to 30 minutes until the bacon is crisp and golden-brown and the sausages are cooked through.

rows.fix: Ensure the pieces are in a single layer with small gaps between them. Increase the oven by 10°C for the final 5 minutes if needed.
rows.cause: Oven too low, or too many pieces crowded together
rows.symptom: Bacon not crisp, still soft after 30 minutes

rows.fix: Stretch the bacon before wrapping and always place seam-side down. If they still unroll, hold with a cocktail stick.
rows.cause: Not stretched sufficiently, or placed seam-side up
rows.symptom: Bacon unrolling from the sausage during cooking

content.text: Pigs in blankets are principally a Christmas dish in Britain, served alongside the turkey with sprouts, roast potatoes, and stuffing. They also appear on the Sunday roast table throughout the year and alongside a full English breakfast. They are almost always the first thing to be eaten and the one item that every cook makes too few of.
